code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test04-DEC-2019Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine, proteinuria), with low urine specific gravity suggesting possible chronic kidney disease. Urinalysis shows protein, glucose, ketones, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Blood glucose and urea are normal; HbA1c is low, possibly due to anemia or recent blood loss. Recommend further evaluation of renal function and possible underlying causes.

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
